narrative:
Cool high pressure is moving into the Northeast U.S., pushing a chilly wedge down the east side of the Appalachians tonight and Wednesday. Tuesday temperatures are on the cool side of average for early April, and Wednesday is even chillier. Temperatures fall below freezing across most of the region tonight, and valleys are frosty again Thursday morning. By Thursday afternoon, temperatures return to seasonal levels, with even warmer weather to follow.

The Monday Message is now available to subscribers, with information on the next week as well as some educational goodies about April weather.

The RaysWeather.Com Photo Contest is underway for photos to appear in the 2027 Blue Ridge Calendar. We are open for submissions through April 15. We broke all records with 2026 calendar sales and look to top that with the 2027 calendar — it's our 24th calendar. Come on! Hit us with your best shot!

The "Mix and Match" Special Offer continues on all the Snowman O'Mometer Pint Glass and Mug (or you can long for a 5 Golfball Day with that pint glass as well). Buy one, and get another drinkware item at 40% off.

Cool; Dry

Last Updated Tue April 07 at 10:31AM by Ray Russell

Cool high pressure is moving into the Northeast U.S., pushing a chilly wedge down the east side of the Appalachians tonight and Wednesday. Tuesday temperatures are on the cool side of average for early April, and Wednesday is even chillier. Temperatures fall below freezing across most of the region tonight, and valleys are frosty again Thursday morning. By Thursday afternoon, temperatures return to seasonal levels, with even warmer weather to follow.

Forecast Discussion

During the next seven days, we transition from a trough in the Eastern U.S. to a Southeastern ridge. Despite that transition, dry conditions remain a constant theme.

Today through Thursday, the center of cool high pressure moves from the western Great Lakes to the Canadian Maritimes. As that occurs, cool air wedges down the east side of the Appalachians tonight and Wednesday.

Tuesday has slightly below-average temperatures, but Wednesday is much cooler. Temperatures fall below freezing across much of the region tonight, and valleys are frosty again Thursday morning. Temperatures moderate back to seasonal levels Thursday afternoon.

Our flow turns more southerly Friday into early next week, and temperatures gradually warm.

A front reaches us from the north Saturday but is pushed back to the north early next week as a ridge builds across the Southeast and then the East Coast. With the front nearby, I cannot completely rule out a rogue afternoon shower Saturday. Warm weather continues into the middle of next week.

Detailed forecast discussion and analysis is only available to RaysWeather supporters.

Monday Message for April 6, 2026

The first full week of April features some chilly conditions to start, but warming up by late week and during the weekend of the 11th-12th. Dry weather will be with us all week.

Apr 06, 2026
Easter Outlook

Easter Sunday will feature a rainy start to the day, but expect some afternoon brightening as clouds begin to decrease. Cooler air will arrive behind a frontal passage.

Next week looks seasonably cool and generally sunny. Frost and freeze concerns will be with us, particularly early Wednesday.
